div.p: Register layout · Syscall ABI · PTRACE_GETREGS output
0x00 (+0) r15 unsigned long long 8 bytes
0x08 (+8) r14 unsigned long long 8 bytes
0x10 (+16) r13 unsigned long long 8 bytes
0x18 (+24) r12 unsigned long long 8 bytes
0x20 (+32) rbp unsigned long long 8 bytes
0x28 (+40) rbx unsigned long long 8 bytes
0x30 (+48) r11 unsigned long long 8 bytes ← clobbered by syscall instr
0x38 (+56) r10 unsigned long long 8 bytes ← ARG 4 ✓ SYSCALL ABI
0x40 (+64) r9 unsigned long long 8 bytes ← ARG 6
0x48 (+72) r8 unsigned long long 8 bytes ← ARG 5
0x50 (+80) rax unsigned long long 8 bytes ← RETURN VALUE (exit stop)
0x58 (+88) rcx unsigned long long 8 bytes ← DESTROYED: syscall saves RIP here
0x60 (+96) rdx unsigned long long 8 bytes ← ARG 3
0x68 (+104) rsi unsigned long long 8 bytes ← ARG 2
0x70 (+112) rdi unsigned long long 8 bytes ← ARG 1
0x78 (+120) orig_rax unsigned long long 8 bytes ← SYSCALL NUMBER (preserved!)
0x80 (+128) rip unsigned long long 8 bytes
0x88 (+136) cs unsigned long long 8 bytes
0x90 (+144) eflags unsigned long long 8 bytes
0x98 (+152) rsp unsigned long long 8 bytes
0xa0 (+160) ss unsigned long long 8 bytes
Total: 168 bytes · PTRACE_GETREGS copies all 168 bytes in one copy_to_user call

orig_rax vs rax — The Preservation Problem At entry stop: rax == orig_rax == syscall_number At exit stop: rax = return_value ← kernel overwrites! orig_rax = syscall_number ← preserved by kernel Rule: always use orig_rax to identify the syscall. Use rax only to read the return value at exit stop. orig_rax = -1 signals "no syscall active" (signal restart logic).

Syscall ABI arg4 = r10 ≠ C ABI arg4 = rcx The syscall instruction clobbers rcx to save the return address (RIP) for the sysret return path. Therefore the kernel CANNOT use rcx for arg4. It uses r10 instead. Reading regs->rcx for arg4 returns the saved RIP — silent wrong data, not a crash. C func call: rdi rsi rdx rcx r8 r9 Syscall ABI: rdi rsi rdx r10 r8 r9

Cache line 0 0x00–0x3F: r15–r10 (args 4 in last 8B) Cache line 1 0x40–0x7F: r9,r8,rax,rcx,rdx,rsi,rdi,orig_rax Cache line 2 0x80–0xBF: rip,cs,eflags,rsp,ss,fs_base Cache line 3 0xC0–0xD7: gs_base,ds,es,fs,gs (last 24B) PTRACE_GETREGS fetches all 168 bytes (3 full + partial line). Hot fields (orig_rax, rax, arg regs) all on cache line 1.

Entry stop (in_syscall == 1 after toggle): Read orig_rax → syscall number Read rdi,rsi,rdx,r10,r8,r9 → arguments 1–6 rax equals orig_rax here (not yet overwritten) Exit stop (in_syscall == 0 after toggle): Read rax → return value (may be negative errno) Read orig_rax → syscall number (still valid) Do NOT trust rdi–r9 (may be clobbered by kernel) Error range: rax ∈ [-4095, -1] → -errno
